questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of citizenship is closely related to the idea of nationality.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Both concepts are associated with the rights and duties of an individual in a particular country.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the assertion and reason are closely related and the reason accurately explains the concept of citizenship.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Citizenship is a form of social contract.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A social contract is an agreement between the government and its citizens, outlining the rights and responsibilities of both parties.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the assertion and reason are both true and the reason accurately explains the concept of a social contract.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A person who has renounced their citizenship cannot vote in their country of origin.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A person who has renounced their citizenship has broken their social contract with their country of origin.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the assertion and reason are both true and the reason accurately explains why a person who has renounced their citizenship cannot vote in their country of origin.
